proposed_hypotheses.text: Cell lines with `braf_v600e=1` have more negative `dependency_BRAF` scores than cell lines with `braf_v600e=0`, after adjusting for lineage, culture type, screen batch, media serum, Cas9 activity, and growth rate.
Cell lines with `egfr_amplification=1` have more negative `dependency_EGFR` scores than cell lines with `egfr_amplification=0`, after the same screen and lineage adjustments.
Cell lines with `erbb2_amplification=1` have more negative `dependency_ERBB2` scores than cell lines with `erbb2_amplification=0`, after the same screen and lineage adjustments.

analyses.result_summary: Adjusted OLS coefficient for `braf_v600e` was -0.5545; BRAF V600E lines were more BRAF-dependent.
Adjusted OLS coefficient for `egfr_amplification` was -0.3490; EGFR-amplified lines were more EGFR-dependent.
Adjusted OLS coefficient for `erbb2_amplification` was -0.4325; ERBB2-amplified lines were more ERBB2-dependent.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Cell lines with `kras_mutation=1` have more negative `dependency_KRAS` scores than cell lines with `kras_mutation=0`, after adjusting for lineage and screen covariates.
Cell lines with `nf1_loss=1` have more negative `dependency_RIT1` scores than cell lines with `nf1_loss=0`, after adjusting for lineage and screen covariates.

analyses.result_summary: Adjusted OLS coefficient for `kras_mutation` was -0.3095; KRAS-mutant lines were substantially more KRAS-dependent.
Adjusted OLS coefficient for `nf1_loss` was -0.0954; NF1-loss lines were more RIT1-dependent.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The `braf_v600e` effect on `dependency_BRAF` is even more negative in `lineage=skin` than in non-skin lineages.
The `egfr_amplification` effect on `dependency_EGFR` is even more negative in `lineage=brain` than in non-brain lineages.
The `erbb2_amplification` effect on `dependency_ERBB2` is even more negative in `lineage=breast` or `lineage=gastric` than in other lineages.
The `kras_mutation` effect on `dependency_KRAS` is even more negative in `lineage=pancreatic` or `lineage=colorectal` than in other lineages.
The `nf1_loss` effect on `dependency_RIT1` is even more negative in `lineage=brain` than in non-brain lineages.

analyses.result_summary: Interaction `braf_v600e:skin` was -0.0128; the skin-specific increment was not significant.
Interaction `egfr_amplification:brain` was -0.0194; the brain-specific increment was not significant.
Interaction `erbb2_amplification:breast_or_gastric` was -0.0254; breast/gastric lines showed a small additional ERBB2 dependency.
Interaction `kras_mutation:pancreatic_or_colorectal` was 0.0023; KRAS-mutant pancreatic/colorectal lines did not have a stronger per-mutation effect.
Interaction `nf1_loss:brain` was -0.0578; NF1-loss brain lines had an additional more-negative RIT1 dependency.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Cell lines with `apc_mutation=1` have more negative `dependency_KIF18A` scores than cell lines with `apc_mutation=0`, after lineage and screen adjustment.
Higher `wnt_activity_score` is associated with more negative `dependency_KIF18A` scores after lineage and screen adjustment.
Within `lineage=colorectal`, the combination `apc_mutation=1` and `wnt_activity_score` in the top quartile has a more negative `dependency_KIF18A` score than expected from either feature alone.

analyses.result_summary: Adjusted OLS coefficient for `apc_mutation` on KIF18A was -0.1469.
Adjusted OLS slope for continuous `wnt_activity_score` on KIF18A was -0.0478 dependency units per score unit.
In colorectal lines, the `apc_mutation:wnt_activity_score_high_q75` interaction was -0.9940, showing strong APC/WNT synergy for KIF18A dependency.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Cell lines satisfying `lineage=colorectal`, `apc_mutation=1`, `wnt_activity_score>=0.819`, and `smad4_loss=0` have much more negative `dependency_KIF18A` scores than all other cell lines.
Among colorectal/APC-mutant/WNT-high cell lines, `smad4_loss=1` suppresses KIF18A dependency, giving less negative `dependency_KIF18A` scores than `smad4_loss=0`.

analyses.result_summary: Complete KIF18A subgroup mean was -1.2990 versus -0.2530 outside the subgroup; n=2571.
Within colorectal/APC-mutant/WNT-high lines, SMAD4-loss mean was -0.2557 versus -1.2990 for SMAD4-intact; the positive effect means suppression of dependency. The three-way interaction was 0.9935.
analyses.code: ttest(dependency_KIF18A, smad4_loss vs intact within colorectal & APC & WNT_Q75)

proposed_hypotheses.text: Cell lines with `copy_number_8q24>=0.428` have more negative `dependency_RPL11` scores than cell lines below that threshold.
Cell lines with `myc_amplification=1` have more negative `dependency_RPL11` scores than cell lines with `myc_amplification=0`, but this effect is largely explained by `copy_number_8q24`.

analyses.result_summary: Top-quartile `copy_number_8q24` lines had mean RPL11 dependency -0.3440 versus -0.2452 outside.
Adjusted continuous `copy_number_8q24` slope was -0.0991 dependency units per copy-number score unit.
Unadjusted `myc_amplification=1` contrast was -0.1091, but after adding `copy_number_8q24` and `rnaseq_MYC_log2_tpm`, the `myc_amplification` coefficient was 0.0092 and not significant.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Cell lines with `rnaseq_MYC_log2_tpm>=5.669` have more negative `dependency_DHX9` scores than cell lines below that threshold.
Higher `growth_rate_doublings_per_day` is associated with more negative `dependency_POLR2A` scores.
Higher `growth_rate_doublings_per_day` is associated with more negative `dependency_TMED10` scores, while `nf1_loss=1` is not a robust TMED10 subgroup after multiple-testing correction.

analyses.result_summary: Top-quartile MYC-expression lines had mean DHX9 dependency -0.3551 versus -0.3265 outside.

After adjusting for `myc_amplification`, `copy_number_8q24`, lineage, and screen covariates, continuous `rnaseq_MYC_log2_tpm` slope was -0.0160; `myc_amplification` itself was -0.0090 and not significant.
Adjusted OLS slope for growth rate on POLR2A was -0.3046; top-quartile growth lines had a mean contrast of -0.1043.

Adjusted OLS slope for growth rate on TMED10 was -0.1010; top-quartile growth contrast was -0.0369. NF1-loss contrast was -0.0137 with raw p=0.0105, but it did not survive the heterogeneity screen FDR.
analyses.code: ols("dependency_TMED10 ~ growth_rate_doublings_per_day + covariates"); screen NF1 subgroup

proposed_hypotheses.text: A systematic one-feature heterogeneity screen over molecular, lineage, screen, and quartile-defined continuous indicators identifies `braf_v600e=1` as the strongest more-negative subgroup for `dependency_BRAF`.
A systematic one-feature heterogeneity screen identifies `egfr_amplification=1` as the strongest more-negative subgroup for `dependency_EGFR`.
A systematic one-feature heterogeneity screen identifies `erbb2_amplification=1` as the strongest more-negative subgroup for `dependency_ERBB2`.
A systematic one-feature heterogeneity screen identifies `kras_mutation=1` as the strongest more-negative subgroup for `dependency_KRAS`.
A systematic one-feature heterogeneity screen identifies `nf1_loss=1` as the strongest more-negative subgroup for `dependency_RIT1`.

analyses.result_summary: Across 72 candidate indicators, the most negative subgroup for `dependency_BRAF` was `braf_v600e=1`: mean -0.8047 inside versus -0.2495 outside; BH q=0.
analyses.code: systematic_screen(binary + categorical levels + continuous Q25/Q75 indicators)

analyses.result_summary: Across 72 candidate indicators, the most negative subgroup for `dependency_EGFR` was `egfr_amplification=1`: mean -0.6010 inside versus -0.2524 outside; BH q=0.
analyses.code: systematic_screen(binary + categorical levels + continuous Q25/Q75 indicators)

analyses.result_summary: Across 72 candidate indicators, the most negative subgroup for `dependency_ERBB2` was `erbb2_amplification=1`: mean -0.6856 inside versus -0.2535 outside; BH q=0.
analyses.code: systematic_screen(binary + categorical levels + continuous Q25/Q75 indicators)

analyses.result_summary: Across 72 candidate indicators, the most negative subgroup for `dependency_KRAS` was `kras_mutation=1`: mean -0.5594 inside versus -0.2520 outside; BH q=0.
analyses.code: systematic_screen(binary + categorical levels + continuous Q25/Q75 indicators)

analyses.result_summary: Across 72 candidate indicators, the most negative subgroup for `dependency_RIT1` was `nf1_loss=1`: mean -0.3527 inside versus -0.2572 outside; BH q=1.12e-66.
analyses.code: systematic_screen(binary + categorical levels + continuous Q25/Q75 indicators)

proposed_hypotheses.text: A systematic one-feature heterogeneity screen identifies `lineage=colorectal` as the strongest more-negative one-feature subgroup for `dependency_KIF18A`, motivating a multi-feature colorectal/APC/WNT/SMAD4 refinement.
A systematic one-feature heterogeneity screen identifies `growth_rate_doublings_per_day` in the top quartile as the strongest more-negative subgroup for `dependency_TMED10`.

A systematic one-feature heterogeneity screen identifies `growth_rate_doublings_per_day` in the top quartile as the strongest more-negative subgroup for `dependency_DHX9`, with high `rnaseq_MYC_log2_tpm` also significant.
A systematic one-feature heterogeneity screen identifies `growth_rate_doublings_per_day` in the top quartile as the strongest more-negative subgroup for `dependency_POLR2A`.

A systematic one-feature heterogeneity screen identifies `myc_amplification=1` as the strongest more-negative subgroup for `dependency_RPL11`, with high `copy_number_8q24` nearly as strong and more explanatory in multivariable models.

analyses.result_summary: Across 72 candidate indicators, the most negative subgroup for `dependency_KIF18A` was `lineage=colorectal`: mean -0.7204 inside versus -0.2524 outside; BH q=0.
analyses.code: systematic_screen(binary + categorical levels + continuous Q25/Q75 indicators)

analyses.result_summary: Across 72 candidate indicators, the most negative subgroup for `dependency_TMED10` was `growth_rate_doublings_per_day>=Q75(0.964)`: mean -0.2816 inside versus -0.2447 outside; BH q=1.81e-22.
analyses.code: systematic_screen(binary + categorical levels + continuous Q25/Q75 indicators)

analyses.result_summary: Across 72 candidate indicators, the most negative subgroup for `dependency_DHX9` was `growth_rate_doublings_per_day>=Q75(0.964)`: mean -0.3614 inside versus -0.3243 outside; BH q=6.15e-23.
analyses.code: systematic_screen(binary + categorical levels + continuous Q25/Q75 indicators)

analyses.result_summary: Across 72 candidate indicators, the most negative subgroup for `dependency_POLR2A` was `growth_rate_doublings_per_day>=Q75(0.964)`: mean -0.4972 inside versus -0.3928 outside; BH q=7.28e-178.
analyses.code: systematic_screen(binary + categorical levels + continuous Q25/Q75 indicators)

analyses.result_summary: Across 72 candidate indicators, the most negative subgroup for `dependency_RPL11` was `myc_amplification=1`: mean -0.3614 inside versus -0.2523 outside; BH q=2.06e-138.
analyses.code: systematic_screen(binary + categorical levels + continuous Q25/Q75 indicators)

proposed_hypotheses.text: For `dependency_KIF18A`, the best-supported final subgroup is `lineage=colorectal`, `apc_mutation=1`, `wnt_activity_score` top quartile, and `smad4_loss=0`, with `smad4_loss=1` disabling the dependency.
For `dependency_RPL11`, the best-supported final subgroup is high `copy_number_8q24`, rather than an independent `myc_amplification` effect.
For `dependency_DHX9`, the best-supported molecular refinement is high `rnaseq_MYC_log2_tpm`, not independent `myc_amplification`.
For `dependency_TMED10`, no tested molecular alteration defines a stronger subgroup than high `growth_rate_doublings_per_day`; the dependency pattern is dominated by a broad growth-associated effect.

analyses.result_summary: Final KIF18A subgroup contrast remained very large: -1.0460; SMAD4 loss within the APC/WNT-high colorectal core shifted the mean by 1.0433 toward non-dependency.
In the multivariable RPL11 model, `copy_number_8q24` remained strongly negative (-0.1043) while `myc_amplification` was not independently negative (0.0092, p=0.169).
In the multivariable DHX9 model, `rnaseq_MYC_log2_tpm` remained negative (-0.0160) while `myc_amplification` was not significant (-0.0090, p=0.176).
TMED10 top heterogeneity hit was high growth rate (contrast -0.0369, BH q=1.81e-22); NF1 loss was small and not FDR-robust.
